title: galium hypocarpium subsp. buxifolium
intro: Galium hypocarpium subsp. buxifolium, also known as Buxberry or Small-leaved Woodruff, is a perennial herb that belongs to the bedstraw family (Rubiaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and as an antiseptic. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Buxberry can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
